title: Add player association to push subscriptions
description: The existing push_subscriptions table has no link to players. Add a player_token column (the fantasy token used in /my-availability/{token} URLs) so notifications can be targeted to specific players. Update the subscribe/unsubscribe endpoints and webpush service to accept and store the player token.
acceptance_criteria: New migration adds player_token TEXT column to push_subscriptions table
Index on player_token for efficient lookups
POST /api/push/subscribe accepts player_token in request body and stores it
POST /api/push/unsubscribe removes subscription for a given player_token + endpoint
New webpush method: GetSubscriptionsByPlayerToken(token string) returns all subscriptions for a player
New webpush method: SendToPlayer(playerToken string, title string, body string, data map[string]string) sends to all of a player's devices
Existing subscriptions without player_token continue to work (column is nullable)
Down migration safely rolls back the column addition

key_decisions: Use player_token (the composite fantasy name token) rather than a numeric player_id, because the availability screen authenticates via token and has no direct player_id
A single player may have multiple subscriptions (phone + laptop), so the relationship is one-to-many
The player_token is the same token used in /my-availability/{token} URLs
existing_infrastructure: webpush.Service already has SaveSubscription, DeleteSubscription, SendNotification

testing_requirements: Verify migration applies cleanly on existing database with existing subscriptions
Test subscribe with player_token stores correctly
Test SendToPlayer sends to all devices for that player
Test down migration rolls back cleanly

context.user_story: As a system, I need to associate push subscriptions with specific players so that captains can send targeted notifications to individual players
context.existing_code: internal/webpush/webpush.go has full CRUD, migrations/003 created the tables
